summary: ALL ordinals decline like adjectives — full gender, number, and case agreement
Hard-stem pattern (-ý/-á/-é): prvý, druhý, štvrtý, piaty, šiesty... stý
Soft-stem exception: tretí/tretia/tretie (3rd) — only one that's soft
Masculine animate Acc = Gen (vidím prvého muža), inanimate Acc = Nom (vidím prvý dom)
Dates use ordinals in GENITIVE: prvého januára, dvadsiateho druhého marca
Time uses ordinals in LOCATIVE with 'o': o druhej, o piatej
Fractions from ordinals: tretina (1/3), štvrtina (1/4), pätina (1/5)
Compound ordinals (21st+): only the last part is ordinal — dvadsiaty prvý
posledný (last), ďalší (next) — also decline like adjectives

rules.title: The Core Idea: Ordinals = Adjectives
rules.text: Every ordinal number in Slovak declines exactly like an adjective. If you know adjective endings, you know ordinal endings. There are only two patterns: hard-stem (-ý) for almost everything, and soft-stem (-í) for 'tretí' (third) only.

rows: prvý muž, prvá žena, prvé dieťa
tretí muž, tretia žena, tretie dieťa

examples: prvý, druhý, štvrtý, piaty... stý — all hard-stem (-ý)

rules.title: Hard-Stem Declension (prvý, druhý, piaty, etc.)
rules.text: This is the pattern for all ordinals except tretí. Same endings as any hard-stem adjective (like dobrý, nový, pekný).
examples: Vidím prvého muža (animate Acc = Gen) vs Vidím prvý dom (inanimate Acc = Nom)
S prvou ženou — With the first woman
O prvom dome — About the first house

rules.title: Soft-Stem Declension (tretí only)
rules.text: Tretí (third) is the only ordinal with soft-stem endings. The differences from hard-stem are small but consistent.
examples: V treťom dome — In the third house

rules.title: All Ordinals 1st–10th
rules.text: The ordinal forms for 1-10. All are hard-stem except tretí. Just learn the stem and add standard adjective endings.

rules.title: Ordinals 11th–20th and Tens
rules.text: Formed from the cardinal stem + -y/-ý. All hard-stem, all follow the same adjective pattern.

rules.title: Compound Ordinals (21st, 22nd, etc.)
rules.text: Only the last part becomes ordinal. Both parts decline in oblique cases.
examples: Instrumental: s dvadsiatym piatym — with the 25th

rules.title: Ordinals in Dates
rules.text: Days of the month use ordinals in GENITIVE case. The month is also in genitive. Years use cardinals.
examples: Narodil sa prvého mája — He was born on May 1st

rules.title: Ordinals in Time
rules.text: Hours use ordinals in LOCATIVE case with the preposition 'o'. The feminine form is used (agreeing with 'hodina').

rules.title: Fractions from Ordinals
rules.text: Fractions are formed by adding -ina to the ordinal stem. They become feminine nouns.
examples: Tretina študentov — A third of the students
Štvrtina hodiny — A quarter of an hour
Polovica chleba — Half of the bread

rules.title: Useful Related Words
rules.text: These position words also decline like adjectives.
examples: Prvá svetová vojna — World War I
Druhá svetová vojna — World War II
